title: Friday, 17/07/2026 Quiz: The Great Knowledge Challenge
description: A challenging but friendly Friday quiz spanning astronomy, science, history, and geography.

questions.question: What physical process supplies the energy radiated by ordinary main-sequence stars such as the Sun?
questions.notes: The Sun fuses hydrogen into helium in its core. Do not accept nuclear fission.

questions.question: A light-year measures what physical quantity?
questions.notes: It is the distance light travels in one year, not a unit of time.

questions.question: What kind of stellar remnant will the Sun ultimately leave after shedding its outer layers?
questions.notes: The expelled outer layers form a planetary nebula; the remnant core is a white dwarf.

questions.question: Name the four terrestrial planets of the Solar System.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order.

questions.question: When astronomers say that a distant galaxy's spectral lines are redshifted, in which direction have their wavelengths moved?
questions.notes: For distant galaxies this is chiefly interpreted in the context of cosmic expansion.

questions.question: Why does a planet move faster in its orbit when it is nearer the Sun?
questions.notes: Accept conservation of angular momentum or Kepler's second law. For a friendly ruling, also accept the general idea that the planet gains speed as it falls closer into the Sun's gravitational field.

questions.question: What type of galaxy is the Milky Way according to its large-scale structure?
questions.notes: A plain 'spiral galaxy' is close, but the central bar is the distinguishing feature requested.

questions.question: Name the four large Galilean moons of Jupiter.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order.

questions.question: The star cluster known in te ao Māori as Matariki is better known internationally by what name?
questions.notes: Matariki is the Māori name commonly used for the Pleiades cluster and for its heliacal rising season.

questions.question: What observational feature of the cosmic microwave background provides evidence that the early universe contained tiny density variations?
questions.notes: These minute variations became the seeds of later large-scale structure.

questions.question: Which law of thermodynamics states that energy cannot be created or destroyed in an isolated system?
questions.notes: This is the thermodynamic expression of energy conservation.

questions.question: Which organelle is the principal site of aerobic cellular respiration in eukaryotic cells?
questions.notes: Accept singular or plural.

questions.question: Name the four nitrogenous bases found in DNA.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order. Uracil belongs in RNA, not DNA.

questions.question: What kind of chemical bond forms when atoms share pairs of electrons?
In a controlled experiment, what is the purpose of a control group?
answers: To provide a baseline for comparison
questions.notes: The control helps isolate the effect of the independent variable.

questions.question: Name the four fundamental interactions of nature.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order. Accept gravitational, electromagnetic, strong, and weak interaction.

questions.question: What process moves fragments of DNA through a gel under an electric field, separating them mainly by size?
questions.notes: Smaller DNA fragments generally migrate farther through the gel.

questions.question: At a subduction zone, why does an oceanic plate usually descend beneath a continental plate?
questions.notes: The greater density of oceanic lithosphere is the key comparison.

questions.question: What term describes the immune system's ability to respond more rapidly and strongly after a second encounter with the same pathogen?
questions.notes: Memory B and T cells underpin this response.

questions.question: What principle says that no two electrons in an atom can have the same complete set of four quantum numbers?
questions.notes: Named for Wolfgang Pauli.

questions.question: Which ancient civilisation developed democracy in the city-state of Athens?
questions.notes: The question refers specifically to Athenian democracy, which excluded much of the population.

questions.question: Which West African empire became renowned for the wealth and pilgrimage of Mansa Musa?
questions.notes: Mansa Musa ruled Mali in the 14th century.

questions.question: Which peace settlement formally ended the Thirty Years' War in 1648?
questions.notes: The broader settlement consisted principally of the treaties of Münster and Osnabrück.

questions.question: At which location was Te Tiriti o Waitangi first signed in February 1840?
answers: Waitangi in the Bay of Islands
questions.notes: The first signing took place at Waitangi on 6 February; copies were later signed around the country.

questions.question: Which Chinese dynasty sponsored Zheng He's major maritime expeditions across the Indian Ocean?
questions.notes: The voyages took place in the early 15th century.

questions.question: Name the three European powers that formed the Triple Entente before the First World War.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order. Accept Britain or the British Empire for the United Kingdom, and the Russian Empire for Russia.

questions.question: What institution, established in 1919, was intended to prevent future wars through collective security but lacked the participation of the United States?
questions.notes: The League was a predecessor in purpose, though not structure, to the United Nations.

questions.question: Name the four empires that collapsed during or in the immediate aftermath of the First World War.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order. Accept German, Habsburg or Austria-Hungary, Ottoman, and Russian or Tsarist empire.

questions.question: From which broad Pacific region did the Polynesian ancestors of Māori migrate to Aotearoa New Zealand?
questions.notes: Current evidence places initial settlement from East Polynesia in the late 13th century.

questions.question: What was the name of the Ottoman levy that recruited boys from Christian communities for imperial service, including service with the Janissaries?
questions.notes: Pronounced approximately dev-SHEER-meh. The practice varied over time and place.

questions.question: Which river flows through or along the borders of more European countries than any other?
questions.notes: The Danube passes through or forms borders of ten countries.

questions.question: Which mountain range forms the conventional geographic boundary between Europe and Asia in Russia?
questions.notes: The Europe-Asia boundary is conventional rather than a plate boundary.

questions.question: Name the three South American countries crossed by the Equator.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order.

questions.question: What climate pattern produces dry conditions on the leeward side of a mountain range after air loses moisture while rising on the windward side?
questions.notes: Accept rain shadow or rain-shadow effect.

questions.question: Aotearoa New Zealand straddles the boundary between which two major tectonic plates?
questions.notes: Both plates are required for full credit.

questions.question: Which sea has no land boundaries and is instead defined by circulating currents in the North Atlantic?
questions.notes: Its boundaries are formed by the North Atlantic subtropical gyre.

questions.question: Which two sovereign states share the island of Hispaniola?
questions.notes: Both countries are required.

questions.question: Name the three widely recognised vertices of the Polynesian Triangle.
questions.notes: Award one point for each; answers may be given in any order. Accept Hawaii, New Zealand or Aotearoa, and Easter Island for Rapa Nui.

questions.question: Which country is entirely surrounded by South Africa?
questions.notes: Lesotho is an enclave; Eswatini also borders Mozambique and is not entirely surrounded.

questions.question: What is the geographic term for a drainage basin whose rivers do not reach the ocean, instead ending in inland lakes, wetlands, or salt flats?
questions.notes: Pronounced roughly en-doh-REE-ik. Accept closed or interior drainage basin.
